title: QUIC RFC 9000 Retry Replay ACK Piggyback Architecture
purpose: Describe the bounded packet-composition floor that prepends fresh pending Initial ACK frames to Retry-selected Initial replay packets without changing Retry token, destination-connection-ID, packet-number, or ClientHello replay semantics.
design_summary: The Retry-selected Initial replay path continues to emit the retained original ClientHello bytes with the Retry source connection ID as the Initial destination connection ID and the Retry token echoed in the Initial token field. During each replay packet composition, the runtime checks Initial ACK-generation state and formats a fresh ACK frame only when a new ack-eliciting Initial packet is pending acknowledgment. Those transient ACK bytes are passed to the existing Initial packet builder as a prefix before the replayed CRYPTO frame. The protected packet is then tracked under its actual next Initial packet number, and ACK state is marked sent only after tracking succeeds. Later Retry-selected Initial recovery probes use the same replay helper, so they retain the Retry-selected keys and token while avoiding duplicate ACK prefixes once the pending ACK has been marked sent.
data_and_state_considerations: ACK prefix bytes are packet-composition data only. They are not stored in the retained ClientHello snapshot, not included in CRYPTO stream replay data, and not allowed to reset packet numbers. Retry token, Retry source connection ID, original destination connection ID, and Initial packet protection ownership remain under the existing retry bootstrap state.
edge_cases_and_constraints: Only Retry-selected Initial replay packets emitted through the retained Retry bootstrap state are in scope.
Ordinary non-Retry bootstrap probe replay remains outside this slice.
No ACK frame is invented when Initial ACK-generation state has no pending ack-eliciting receipt.
A sent ACK prefix suppresses duplicate ACK-only or repeated piggyback triggers for the same pending Initial acknowledgment.
The replayed CRYPTO frame must preserve the retained ClientHello bytes and stream offsets after any ACK prefix.
The packet number space is not reset after Retry.
alternatives_considered: Treat live Initial CRYPTO ACK piggyback coverage as sufficient; rejected because Retry-selected replay uses a retained ClientHello snapshot, Retry token, and Retry-selected Initial keys.
Store ACK bytes in the retained ClientHello snapshot; rejected because ACK frames are not reliable CRYPTO data and must not be replayed as handshake bytes.
Widen the slice to ordinary non-Retry bootstrap probe replay and interop success; rejected because those are separate scheduling and external-runner claims.
Mark ACK state sent before packet tracking; rejected because suppressed sends must not consume ACK-generation state.
risks: General delayed-ACK timer behavior remains unclaimed.
Ordinary non-Retry bootstrap probe replay ACK piggyback behavior remains unclaimed until separately traced.
Endpoint interop success and public API behavior remain outside this architecture slice.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This design covers fresh pending Initial ACK prefixing for Retry-selected Initial replay packets and later Retry-selected Initial recovery probes only. It does not claim ordinary non-Retry bootstrap probe replay, candidate-path or multipath scheduling, endpoint interop success, public API behavior, or a general delayed-ACK timer.
